item.title: backlog add and edit have no --step flag for setting implementation steps
item.intent: Users must be able to add and replace implementation steps via CLI without hand-editing JSON — steps are required for sprint readiness and a missing flag forces a manual workaround.
item.description: backlog add creates items with steps: [] and backlog edit has no flag to append or replace steps. The only way to populate the steps array is to edit the backlog JSON directly, which violates the CLI-first design. Both commands already follow a consistent flag pattern (--ac for AC entries, --scope for scope patterns). Steps should follow the same pattern: --step adds one step (repeatable), --clear-steps resets the array. This unblocks grooming items without requiring JSON editing.
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add --step 'Do X' creates an item with steps: ["Do X"]
backlog add --step 'Do X' --step 'Do Y' creates steps in order
backlog edit <ID> --step 'Do X' appends X to the existing steps array
backlog edit <ID> --clear-steps resets steps to empty array
